service: Create public spaces to facilitate livability.
oneLiner: This work matters because DOT's Plaza Program, launched in 2008, turns underused street space into public plazas using fast, low-cost "quick-build" methods, bringing open space to neighborhoods - often lower-income ones - that otherwise lack it.
summary: The Plaza Program grew out of PlaNYC 2030, the city's 2007 long-range sustainability plan, which set a goal of every New Yorker living within a ten-minute walk of a park. Rather than waiting years for costly capital construction, DOT under Commissioner Janette Sadik-Khan used movable barriers and tactical-urbanism techniques to convert street space into plazas almost overnight, first tested in 2006-07 and formalized as a program in 2008. Local nonprofit partners help choose sites and maintain the resulting spaces, with the program prioritizing high-pedestrian, low-income neighborhoods that otherwise have little accessible open space.

sources.label: NYC DOT Launches New Public Plaza Program - PPS
